ucs.title: [LLM] Aquatic Panda (G0143) Log4Shell exploitation: VMware Horizon Tomcat spawning PowerShell/cmd recon chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Aquatic Panda intrusion vector documented by CrowdStrike (Dec 2021): exploitation of CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) against internet-facing VMware Horizon servers, where the Apache Tomcat service (ws_TomcatService.exe) spawns PowerShell/cmd to perform host enumeration (whoami, ipconfig, net group 'domain admins'). This parent-child tree is the defining IOC of Aquatic Panda's initial access on Horizon estates.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as child_procs from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("ws_TomcatService.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","javaw.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmic.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") AND (Processes.parent_process_path="*VMware*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*Horizon*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*Tomcat*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(process_name) as distinct_children by dest | where distinct_children>=2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("ws_TomcatService.exe", "tomcat.exe", "tomcat9.exe", "java.exe", "javaw.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any ("VMware", "Horizon", "Tomcat")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmic.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
| extend SuspIndicator = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("domain admins","DownloadString","IEX","-enc","FromBase64String","hxxp","http"), "high",
    "medium")
| summarize ChildProcs = make_set(FileName), CmdLines = make_set(ProcessCommandLine), Count = count(), FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, AccountName
| where array_length(ChildProcs) >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: ws_TomcatService.exe is a static, low-volume process unique to VMware Horizon Connection Servers. It has essentially zero legitimate reason to spawn cmd/PowerShell or built-in recon binaries. CrowdStrike's public Aquatic Panda case study identifies precisely this chain (Tomcat → cmd → whoami/ipconfig/net group) as the actor's calling card during their late-2021 Horizon intrusions, distinguishing t

ucs.title: [LLM] Aquatic Panda (G0143) LSASS credential theft via comsvcs.dll MiniDump LOLBin
ucs.description: Detects Aquatic Panda's documented LSASS dumping technique using rundll32 to invoke the MiniDump (ordinal #24) export of comsvcs.dll — a fileless living-off-the-land method preferred by this China-nexus group (per CrowdStrike) over Mimikatz to evade AV signatures while staging credentials for lateral movement to telecom/tech targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*" OR Processes.process="*,24*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe"))
| where ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump", "#24", " 24 ", ",24 ", " 24,")
| extend TargetIsLsass = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass", "624", "\"lsass.exe\"") or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"\b\d{2,5}\b\s+[A-Za-z]:\\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, TargetIsLsass
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: The `rundll32 comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> <path> full` pattern has no benign administrative use — the export is undocumented and only invoked by attackers. CrowdStrike's Aquatic Panda incident report explicitly calls out this LOLBin pattern as part of the actor's hands-on-keyboard credential staging after Log4Shell footholds, making it a high-confidence indicator of this specific China-nexus opera
